name: Zerodha Trading gRPC Schema
scenario: Zerodha's order routing system needs to call 5 internal microservices with <0.5ms total latency. Define the gRPC proto schema for the trading system, ensuring backward compatibility for rolling deployments.

core.description: OrderRequest message: accountId (string), symbol (string), exchange (Exchange enum), quantity (int32), limitPricePaise (int64), type (OrderType enum), idempotencyKey (string). All fields numbered correctly starting at 1.
OrderResponse message: orderId (string), status (OrderStatus enum), fillPricePaise (int64), rejectionReason (string), placedAt (google.protobuf.Timestamp).
3 enums with UNSPECIFIED=0: OrderType (MARKET, LIMIT, STOP_LOSS), OrderStatus (PENDING, PLACED, FILLED, REJECTED), Exchange (NSE, BSE, NFO).
TradeService with 2 RPCs: PlaceOrder (unary) and GetOrderHistory (server-streaming → returns stream OrderResponse).
Backward compatibility: field 5 was 'product_code' — add reserved 5; reserved 'product_code'; to prevent reuse.
Java builder demonstration: build an OrderRequest using simulated/generated newBuilder() pattern with all fields set.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): builder creates correct message, reserved field not reusable (documented), stream RPC signature documented. All 3 pass.
README: explains when to use gRPC vs REST vs GraphQL with Zerodha as the example.

bonus.description: MarketDataService: GetQuote (unary) + WatchQuotes (server-streaming). Quote message with LTP, bid, ask, volume, Timestamp.
PortfolioService: GetPortfolio (returns stream Position). Position message with symbol, quantity, avgBuyPrice, unrealisedPnl.
Serialization size comparison: Java code that compares simulated proto byte count vs JSON string length for same OrderRequest data. Assert proto < JSON.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Field numbers are permanent: once assigned, a field number can NEVER be reused for a different field — even if the original field is removed. Use 'reserved N;' and 'reserved name;' for removed fields. Enum first value MUST be 0 in proto3: name it UNSPECIFIED or UNKNOWN (e.g., ORDER_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED = 0). java_multiple_files = true: generates one .java file per message — cleaner than one giant file. stream keyword in service: 'returns (stream OrderResponse)' means the server sends 0..N messages before closing. Field numbers 1-15: encoded in 1 byte — use these for the most commonly sent fields.
